free_text: 116Sn,122Sn data
Hole states in the tin isotopes observed by the (p,t)
reaction
The Michigan State University
isochronous cyclotron.
Solid state detector telescope
NaI monitor detector at a fixed laboratory
angle of 90 degree.
Enge split-pole spectrograph
The absolute cross section was obtained by measuring
the elastic scattering at 8., 10., and 12. degr. and
comparing with an optical model calculation.
Calibration was performed using the known low lying
states of the lighter tin isotopes, the oxygen and
carbon impurities in the tin targets and also the
58Ni(p,t) and 64Ni(p,t) reactions.
The absolute cross sections are
accurate to better than 10%, one of the largest
uncertainties in extracting the cross section for the
broad feature arises from the background subtraction.
An estimate of this error was made by repeating the
extraction of the peak a number of times with
different backgrounds.
DR

REACTION code and E-EXC was added to COMMON in
Subents 2-9. Subents 10,11 were added.
The targets were self-supporting
foils of various thicknesses ranging from about 5.3 to
0.3 mg/cm2 with the following isotopic abundances:
